content:
# 1 Peter Chapter 1 — The SOPHIA Bible (Consensus)
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA
**Source Manuscript:** Codex Sinaiticus (Aleph, 01), pages 265-266

1. Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ, to the chosen sojourners of the dispersion in Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia,

2. according to the foreknowledge of God the Father, in the sanctification of the Spirit, for obedience and sprinkling of the blood of Jesus Christ: Grace to you and peace be multiplied.

3. Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who according to his great mercy begot us again into a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead,

4. into an inheritance incorruptible and undefiled and unfading, kept in heaven for you,

5. who by the power of God are being guarded through faith for a salvation ready to be revealed in the last time.

6. In this you rejoice greatly, though now for a little while, if it must be, you have been grieved by various trials,

7. so that the tested genuineness of your faith — more precious than gold that perishes though tested by fire — may be found unto praise and glory and honor at the revelation of Jesus Christ,

8. whom, not having seen, you love; in whom, though you do not now see him, yet believing, you rejoice with joy inexpressible and filled with glory,

9. receiving the outcome of your faith — the salvation of your souls.

10. Concerning this salvation, the prophets who prophesied about the grace intended for you searched and inquired diligently,

11. searching into what person or what time the Spirit of Christ within them was indicating when it testified beforehand the sufferings destined for Christ and the glories to follow.

12. To whom it was revealed that not to themselves but to you they were ministering these things, which have now been announced to you through those who proclaimed the gospel to you by the Holy Spirit sent from heaven — things into which angels desire to look.

13. Therefore, girding up the loins of your mind, being sober, set your hope fully upon the grace being brought to you at the revelation of Jesus Christ.

14. As children of obedience, do not be conformed to the former desires of your ignorance,

15. but as he who called you is holy, you also become holy in all your conduct,

16. because it is written, "You shall be holy, for I am holy."

17. And if you call upon the Father who judges impartially according to each one's work, conduct yourselves in fear during the time of your sojourning,

18. knowing that you were redeemed from the futile conduct handed down from your fathers, not with perishable things such as silver or gold,

19. but with the precious blood of Christ, as of a lamb without blemish and without spot,

20. who was foreknown indeed before the foundation of the world but was made manifest in the last times for your sake,

21. who through him are believers in God, who raised him from the dead and gave him glory, so that your faith and hope are in God.

22. Having purified your souls by your obedience to the truth for genuine brotherly love, love one another earnestly from a pure heart,

23. having been born again, not of perishable seed but of imperishable, through the living and abiding word of God.

24. For "all flesh is as grass, and all its glory as the flower of grass. The grass withered, and the flower fell away,

25. but the word of the Lord abides forever." And this is the word that was proclaimed to you as good news.

---
## Consensus Notes

**Method:** SOPHIA evaluated each verse across three independent translations — EMET (literal), LOGOS (formal), and PNEUMA (dynamic) — selecting readings that best preserve the sense and structure of the Sinaiticus Greek while remaining clear in English. Where all three agreed substantively, the formal register was preferred. Where they diverged, the majority reading was adopted unless a minority reading better served both accuracy and clarity.

**v.1 — "chosen sojourners":** EMET's "chosen sojourners" and LOGOS's "elect sojourners" both render eklektois parepidemois faithfully. PNEUMA's expansion to "God's chosen ones, living as strangers scattered" captures the sense dynamically but adds words not in the Greek. Consensus adopts "chosen sojourners" as precise and recognizable.

**v.3 — "begot us again":** EMET reads "having begotten us again," LOGOS "begat us again," PNEUMA "gave us a completely new birth." Consensus uses "begot us again" as a clear English rendering of anagennesas that preserves the single-verb construction.

**v.7 — "tested genuineness":** LOGOS's "tested genuineness of your faith" best captures to dokimion as the proven quality (not the process). EMET's "testing of the faith" conflates process and result. PNEUMA paraphrases. Consensus follows LOGOS.

**v.9 — "outcome" vs. "end":** EMET reads "end" (telos), LOGOS and PNEUMA read "outcome" / "goal." Consensus adopts "outcome" — telos here means the intended result, not termination.

**v.13 — "girding up the loins of your mind":** EMET and LOGOS preserve the metaphor from Exodus 12:11; PNEUMA renders it "prepare your minds for action." Consensus retains the literal image, which is theologically significant and well-known.

**v.18 — "redeemed" vs. "ransomed":** EMET reads "redeemed" (elutrothete), LOGOS reads "ransomed," PNEUMA reads "set free." Consensus uses "redeemed" as the traditional English equivalent that covers both the theological and commercial senses of lutroo.

**v.22 — "from a pure heart":** EMET uniquely preserves "from a pure heart" (ek katharas kardias), which is present in Sinaiticus. LOGOS reads "from the heart." PNEUMA reads "from the heart." Consensus includes "pure" as it reflects the manuscript reading.

**v.24 — "For" vs. "because":** EMET opens with "because" (dioti/hoti), LOGOS and PNEUMA with "For." Consensus adopts "For" as the standard English rendering of the causal conjunction introducing a scripture citation.

— SOPHIA, Arbiter
